channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Snapchat’s failure to protect kids revealed in New Mexico lawsuit
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico’s attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: New update says M4 chip-based MacBook Pro, iMac, and redesigned Mac mini will launch in 2024 - with an October reveal looking likely
item.description: Apple should be releasing its new M4 chip-powered Macs before 2025 - though details surrounding the debut of the next-gen Macs have been vague so far.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Skoda unveils the all-electric Elroq SUV, with specs and price to tempt buyers away from combustion engines
item.description: Skoda's latest EV offering is priced to take on some of the most affordable electric cars in the market, while staying true to the brand's vision of quality and clever touches.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Ring Protect is history - enter the era of Ring Home subscription (plus five new features)
item.description: We're still a long way off from the launch of the Samsung Galaxy S26, but the base model may be dropped.
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: The role of IoT in shaping smart cities
item.description: As people increasingly migrate from rural areas to cities for better economic opportunities and improved living standards, modern municipalities are faced with challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Diez maneras de ‘clavar’ la entrevista al puesto de CIO
item.description: Ha trabajado duro para progresar en su carrera y está listo para dar el salto al liderazgo. Ha conseguido una entrevista para un puesto ejecutivo de TI en una empresa que le gusta.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Huawei's Vision for AI-Powered Digitalisation: Amplifying Intelligence in Industries Globally
item.description: Huawei's grand vision to accelerate the integration of artificial intelligence into industries globally, driving transformation and efficiency through amplified intelligence, digitalisation, and collaborative partnerships.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sonos outlines a plan to win back user trust after its app fiasco
item.description: Speaker company Sonos laid out a number of new plans and commitments on Tuesday in an attempt to turn around the beleaguered sound company following a bug-riddled app relaunch in May that’s still impacting the company.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: California governor vetoes controversial AI safety bill
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom on Sunday vetoed a hotly contested artificial intelligence safety bill after the tech industry raised objections, saying it could drive AI companies from the state and hinder innovation.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: For Fiscal Service, intelligent automation transforms federal workflows
item.description: At the Bureau of the Fiscal Service, automation is helping to reduce manual workloads, and to increase employee efficiency and morale. The post For Fiscal Service, intelligent automation transforms federal workflows first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Dana Sukontarak, Federal News Network

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat has failed to act on rampant reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Epic Games is suing Google and Samsung for this phone setting
item.description: Video game maker Epic Games sued Google and Samsung on Monday, accusing the tech companies of coordinating to block third-party competition in application distribution on Samsung devices.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Windows 11 Insider Previews: What’s in the latest build?
item.description: The Windows 11 2023 Update has been released, but behind the scenes, Microsoft is constantly working to improve the newest version of Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The 2024 election is razor close. TikTok creators could tip it
item.description: If Barack Obama’s digital journey to the White House in 2008 marked the Facebook election, and Donald Trump’s social media presence in 2016 made that race the Twitter election, then 2024 seems likely to be remembered as the TikTok election.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Regulators and fintech innovators in Africa need to talk more
item.description: The African continent looks set to realize around $230 billion in financial sector revenue by 2025, despite various political and economic challenges, according to a McKinsey report on Africa’s evolving fintech industry.
item.dc:creator: Olugbenga Agboola, Fast Company

item.title: FAA confirms SpaceX Falcon 9 is grounded because of Crew-9 launch issue
item.description: The agency is investigating why the rocket's second stage missed its deorbit landing target zone after this weekend's Crew-9 launch.
item.dc:creator: Richard Tribou, The Mercury News

item.title: Inside the trials and tribulations of a major ransomware attack
item.description: Like many small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs), ‘JPX Construction’ didn’t see itself as a potential target for a ransomware attack. It wasn’t a high-profile organisation. It didn’t seem to hold much high-value data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Two good Visual Studio Code alternatives
item.description: The conductor of my choir famously tells us singers, “I only want everything all the time.” Well, as a developer, my mantra for code editors and IDEs (integrated development environments) is exactly that: I only want everything all the time.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The worst programmer I know
item.description: If all code is legacy code, then we are all maintainers. I carry an image in my head of a vast sea of silent, diligent, dedicated coders who silently maintain much of the code that makes the world go round. I carry this image because I am one of those coders.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Breaking through AI data bottlenecks
item.description: As AI models become increasingly commoditized, the data required to train and fine-tune them has never been more critical.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Verizon customers report major outage on mobile network
item.description: Tens of thousands of Verizon users reported outages for the wireless carrier’s services across the U.S. on Monday, with Chicago and Indianapolis among the hardest-hit cities and some iPhone users stuck in “SOS” mode. According to tracking website Downdetector.com, the outage began at around 9:30 a.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cyber pay in government is as fragmented as ever
item.description: It’s a case of “haves and have-nots.” Here’s an informal run down of agencies with special cyber pay authorities, including DoD, DHS, and the VA. The post Cyber pay in government is as fragmented as ever first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Helene and other storms dumped a whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain on the South
item.description: Meteorologists calculate that more than 40 trillion gallons of rain drenched the Southeast United States in the last week from Hurricane Helene and a run-of-the-mill rainstorm that sloshed in ahead of it.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: X's value has fallen 79% since Musk takeover, Fidelity estimates
item.description: Fidelity, which originally invested $19.66 million, now values its stake in the company at $4.19 million.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: 6 ways to apply automation in devsecops
item.description: The goal of devsecops is to integrate security throughout the software development life cycle. To do this, the security team can’t focus on just the most obvious software development vulnerabilities or just the processes that are the easiest to secure.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk faces $1.9 million fine to end X ban in Brazil
item.description: Brazil's Supreme Court said that the social platform X, owned by Elon Musk, owes a fine of 10 million reais, which is about $1.9 million, to end its ban in the country, building off a separate $3.3 million fine previously ordered by the court, Reuters reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: DoD seeks to create ‘culture of learning’ through CDAO
item.description: Christopher Skaluba, the executive director in the DoD CDAO, said the office is creating tools to provide data-driven insights on AI workforce needs. The post DoD seeks to create ‘culture of learning’ through CDAO first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Zero Trust + AI: A match made in the clouds
item.description: Have any terms in the cybersecurity space generated more buzz than “zero trust” and “AI”? At the risk of sounding like a Magic 8 Ball, my reply is no.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ruby on Rails streamlines deployment with Kamal 2
item.description: Ruby on Rails 8, the latest version of the well-known web app framework for the Ruby programming language, has reached the beta stage. The update promises to make deployment of Rails web apps friendly and usable, without developers needing to rely on a platform-as-a-service (PaaS) cloud setup.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Make these changes now to protect your kids online
item.description: As your teenagers navigate social media, here are some ways you can protect them including through adjusting settings, enforcing limits and communicating honestly about the dangers that exist online.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police are probing apparent cyber vandalism on Wi-Fi networks at UK train stations
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. transport officials and police said Thursday they are investigating a “cyber-security incident” after users of public Wi-Fi networks at the country’s biggest railway stations reported being shown anti-Muslim messages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
